32			},

50				expected: "fonts",
51			},

56			},

62				expected: "chrome",
63			},
64			{

67				expected: "chrome",
68			},

72				expected: "chrome",

111				expected: "deno",

190	// TestGetDomainEcosystem_Determinism verifies that GetDomainEcosystem returns the same result
191	// across repeated calls for domains that exist in multiple ecosystems (e.g. cdn.jsdelivr.net
192	// is in both "node" and "node-cdns" and must always resolve to "node-cdns").
193	func TestGetDomainEcosystem_Determinism(t *testing.T) {
194		cases := []struct {
195			domain   string
196			expected string
197		}{
198			{"cdn.jsdelivr.net", "node-cdns"},
199			{"crates.io", "rust"}, // also appears in python ecosystem
200			{"index.crates.io", "rust"},
201			{"static.crates.io", "rust"},
202		}
203		for _, c := range cases {
204			for i := range 20 {
205				got := GetDomainEcosystem(c.domain)
206				if got != c.expected {
207					t.Errorf("call %d: GetDomainEcosystem(%q) = %q, want %q", i, c.domain, got, c.expected)
208				}
209			}
210		}
211	}

142	// getEcosystemDomains returns the domains for a given ecosystem category.
143	// Supports compound ecosystem identifiers (see compoundEcosystems).
144	// The returned list is sorted and contains unique entries.
145	func getEcosystemDomains(category string) []string {
146		// Check for compound ecosystem first
147		if components, ok := compoundEcosystems[category]; ok {
148			domainMap := make(map[string]bool)

163		if !exists {

166		// Return a sorted copy to avoid external modification

168		copy(result, domains)

211			}

213			// Get domains for this ecosystem

215			if len(domains) > 0 {

217				for _, d := range domains {
218					domainMap[d] = true
219				}
220			}
221		}

223		// Convert map to sorted slice
224		result := make([]string, 0, len(domainMap))
225		for domain := range domainMap {
226			result = append(result, domain)
227		}
228		sort.Strings(result)

233	// GetAllowedDomains returns the allowed domains from network permissions.
234	//
235	// # Behavior based on network permissions configuration:
236	//
237	//  1. No network permissions (nil):
238	//     Returns default ecosystem domains for backwards compatibility.
239	//
240	//  2. Allowed list with "defaults" only:
241	//     network: defaults  OR  network: { allowed: [defaults] }
242	//     Returns default ecosystem domains.
243	//
244	//  3. Allowed list with multiple ecosystems:
245	//     network:
246	//     allowed:
247	//     - defaults
248	//     - github
249	//     Processes the Allowed list, expanding all ecosystem identifiers and merging them.
250	//
251	//  4. Allowed list with custom domains:
252	//     network:
253	//     allowed:
254	//     - example.com
255	//     - python
256	//     Processes the Allowed list, expanding ecosystem identifiers.
257	//
258	//  5. Empty Allowed list (deny-all):
259	//     network: {}  OR  network: { allowed: [] }
260	//     Returns empty slice (no network access).
261	//
262	// The returned list is sorted and deduplicated.
263	//
264	// # Supported ecosystem identifiers:
265	//   - "defaults": basic infrastructure (certs, JSON schema, Ubuntu, package mirrors)
266	//   - "chrome": headless Chrome/Puppeteer browser testing (*.google.com, *.googleapis.com, *.gvt1.com)
267	//   - "clojure": Clojure/Clojars
268	//   - "containers": container registries (Docker, GHCR, etc.)
269	//   - "dart": Dart/Flutter ecosystem
270	//   - "deno": Deno runtime (deno.land, *.jsr.io, googleapis.deno.dev, fresh.deno.dev)
271	//   - "dotnet": .NET and NuGet ecosystem
272	//   - "elixir": Elixir/Hex
273	//   - "github": GitHub domains (*.githubusercontent.com, github.githubassets.com, etc.)
274	//   - "github-actions": GitHub Actions blob storage domains
275	//   - "go": Go ecosystem
276	//   - "haskell": Haskell ecosystem
277	//   - "java": Java/Maven/Gradle
278	//   - "kotlin": Kotlin/JetBrains
279	//   - "linux-distros": Linux distribution package repositories
280	//   - "node": Node.js/NPM/Yarn
281	//   - "perl": Perl/CPAN
282	//   - "php": PHP/Composer
283	//   - "playwright": Playwright testing framework
284	//   - "python": Python/PyPI/Conda
285	//   - "ruby": Ruby/RubyGems
286	//   - "rust": Rust/Cargo/Crates
287	//   - "scala": Scala/SBT
288	//   - "swift": Swift/CocoaPods
289	//   - "terraform": HashiCorp/Terraform
290	//   - "zig": Zig
291	func GetAllowedDomains(network *NetworkPermissions) []string {
292		if network == nil {
293			domainsLog.Print("No network permissions specified, using defaults")
294			return getEcosystemDomains("defaults") // Default allow-list for backwards compatibility
295		}
296	
297		// Handle empty allowed list (deny-all case)
298		if len(network.Allowed) == 0 {
299			domainsLog.Print("Empty allowed list, denying all network access")
300			return []string{} // Return empty slice, not nil
301		}
302	
303		domainsLog.Printf("Processing %d allowed domains/ecosystems", len(network.Allowed))
304	
305		// Process the allowed list, expanding ecosystem identifiers if present
306		// Use a map to deduplicate domains
307		domainMap := make(map[string]bool)
308		for _, domain := range network.Allowed {
309			// Try to get domains for this ecosystem category
310			ecosystemDomains := getEcosystemDomains(domain)
311			if len(ecosystemDomains) > 0 {
312				// This was an ecosystem identifier, expand it
313				domainsLog.Printf("Expanded ecosystem '%s' to %d domains", domain, len(ecosystemDomains))
314				for _, d := range ecosystemDomains {
315					domainMap[d] = true
316				}
317			} else {
318				// Add the domain as-is (regular domain name)
319				domainMap[domain] = true
320			}
321		}
322	
323		// Convert map to sorted slice
324		expandedDomains := make([]string, 0, len(domainMap))
325		for domain := range domainMap {
326			expandedDomains = append(expandedDomains, domain)
327		}
328		sort.Strings(expandedDomains)
329	
330		return expandedDomains
331	}

333	// ecosystemPriority defines the order in which ecosystems are checked by GetDomainEcosystem.
334	// More specific sub-ecosystems are listed before their parent ecosystems so that domains
335	// shared between multiple ecosystems resolve deterministically to the most specific one.
336	// For example, "node-cdns" is listed before "node" so that cdn.jsdelivr.net returns "node-cdns".
337	// All known ecosystems are enumerated here; any ecosystem not in this list is checked last
338	// in sorted order (for forward-compatibility with new entries).
339	var ecosystemPriority = []string{
340		"node-cdns", // before "node" — more specific CDN sub-ecosystem
341		"rust",      // before "python" — crates.io/index.crates.io/static.crates.io are native Rust domains
342		"clojure",
343		"containers",
344		"dart",
345		"defaults",
346		"dev-tools",
347		"deno", // before "node" — deno-specific domains take precedence over the broader node set
348		"dotnet",
349		"elixir",
350		"fonts", // before "chrome" — fonts.googleapis.com is a fonts domain, not a chrome domain
351		"github",
352		"github-actions",
353		"go",
354		"haskell",
355		"java", // before "chrome" — maven.google.com and dl.google.com are Java domains, not chrome domains
356		"chrome",
357		"kotlin",
358		"linux-distros",
359		"local",
360		"node",
361		"perl",
362		"php",
363		"playwright",
364		"python",
365		"ruby",
366		"scala",
367		"swift",
368		"terraform",
369		"zig",
370		"default-safe-outputs", // compound: defaults + dev-tools + github + local
371	}

373	// GetDomainEcosystem returns the ecosystem identifier for a given domain, or empty string if not found.
374	// Ecosystems are checked in ecosystemPriority order so that the result is deterministic even when
375	// a domain appears in multiple ecosystems (e.g. cdn.jsdelivr.net is in both "node" and "node-cdns").
376	func GetDomainEcosystem(domain string) string {
377		checked := make(map[string]bool, len(ecosystemPriority))
378	
379		// Check ecosystems in priority order first
380		for _, ecosystem := range ecosystemPriority {
381			checked[ecosystem] = true
382			domains := getEcosystemDomains(ecosystem)
383			for _, ecosystemDomain := range domains {
384				if matchesDomain(domain, ecosystemDomain) {
385					return ecosystem
386				}
387			}
388		}
389	
390		// Fall back to any ecosystems not in the priority list, sorted for determinism
391		remaining := make([]string, 0)
392		for ecosystem := range ecosystemDomains {
393			if !checked[ecosystem] {
394				remaining = append(remaining, ecosystem)
395			}
396		}
397		sort.Strings(remaining)
398		for _, ecosystem := range remaining {
399			domains := getEcosystemDomains(ecosystem)
400			for _, ecosystemDomain := range domains {
401				if matchesDomain(domain, ecosystemDomain) {
402					return ecosystem
403				}
404			}
405		}
406	
407		return "" // No ecosystem found
408	}

410	// matchesDomain checks if a domain matches a pattern (supports wildcards)
411	func matchesDomain(domain, pattern string) bool {
412		// Exact match
413		if domain == pattern {
414			return true
415		}
416	
417		// Wildcard match
418		if strings.HasPrefix(pattern, "*.") {
419			suffix := pattern[2:] // Remove "*."
420			return strings.HasSuffix(domain, "."+suffix) || domain == suffix
421		}
422	
423		return false
424	}

426	// extractHTTPMCPDomains extracts domain names from HTTP MCP server URLs in tools configuration
427	// Returns a slice of domain names (e.g., ["mcp.tavily.com", "api.example.com"])
428	func extractHTTPMCPDomains(tools map[string]any) []string {
429		if tools == nil {
430			return []string{}
431		}
432	
433		domains := []string{}
434	
435		// Iterate through tools to find HTTP MCP servers
436		for toolName, toolConfig := range tools {
437			configMap, ok := toolConfig.(map[string]any)
438			if !ok {
439				// Tool has no explicit config (e.g., github: null means local mode)
440				continue
441			}
442	
443			// Special handling for GitHub MCP in remote mode
444			// When mode: remote is set, the URL is implicitly the hosted GitHub Copilot MCP server
445			if toolName == "github" {
446				if modeField, hasMode := configMap["mode"]; hasMode {
447					if modeStr, ok := modeField.(string); ok && modeStr == "remote" {
448						domainsLog.Printf("Detected GitHub MCP remote mode, adding %s to domains", constants.GitHubCopilotMCPDomain)
449						domains = append(domains, constants.GitHubCopilotMCPDomain)
450						continue
451					}
452				}
453			}
454	
455			// Check if this is an HTTP MCP server
456			mcpType, hasType := configMap["type"].(string)
457			url, hasURL := configMap["url"].(string)
458	
459			// HTTP MCP servers have either type: http or just a url field
460			isHTTPMCP := (hasType && mcpType == "http") || (!hasType && hasURL)
461	
462			if isHTTPMCP && hasURL {
463				// Extract domain from URL (e.g., "https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp/" -> "mcp.tavily.com")
464				domain := stringutil.ExtractDomainFromURL(url)
465				if domain != "" {
466					domainsLog.Printf("Extracted HTTP MCP domain '%s' from tool '%s'", domain, toolName)
467					domains = append(domains, domain)
468				}
469			}
470		}
471	
472		return domains
473	}

492	// mergeDomainsWithNetworkToolsAndRuntimes combines default domains with NetworkPermissions, HTTP MCP server domains, and runtime ecosystem domains
493	// Returns a deduplicated, sorted, comma-separated string suitable for AWF's --allow-domains flag
494	func mergeDomainsWithNetworkToolsAndRuntimes(defaultDomains []string, network *NetworkPermissions, tools map[string]any, runtimes map[string]any) string {
495		domainMap := make(map[string]bool)
496	
497		// Add default domains
498		for _, domain := range defaultDomains {
499			domainMap[domain] = true
500		}
501	
502		// Add NetworkPermissions domains (if specified)
503		if network != nil && len(network.Allowed) > 0 {
504			// Expand ecosystem identifiers and add individual domains
505			expandedDomains := GetAllowedDomains(network)
506			for _, domain := range expandedDomains {
507				domainMap[domain] = true
508			}
509		}
510	
511		// Add HTTP MCP server domains (if tools are specified)
512		if tools != nil {
513			mcpDomains := extractHTTPMCPDomains(tools)
514			for _, domain := range mcpDomains {
515				domainMap[domain] = true
516			}
517		}
518	
519		// Add Playwright ecosystem domains (if Playwright tool is specified)
520		// This ensures browser binaries can be downloaded when Playwright initializes
521		if tools != nil {
522			playwrightDomains := extractPlaywrightDomains(tools)
523			for _, domain := range playwrightDomains {
524				domainMap[domain] = true
525			}
526		}
527	
528		// Add runtime ecosystem domains (if runtimes are specified)
529		if runtimes != nil {
530			runtimeDomains := getDomainsFromRuntimes(runtimes)
531			for _, domain := range runtimeDomains {
532				domainMap[domain] = true
533			}
534		}
535	
536		// Convert to sorted slice for consistent output
537		domains := make([]string, 0, len(domainMap))
538		for domain := range domainMap {
539			domains = append(domains, domain)
540		}
541		sort.Strings(domains)
542	
543		// Join with commas for AWF --allow-domains flag
544		return strings.Join(domains, ",")
545	}

649	// GetAPITargetDomains returns the set of domains to add to the allow-list when engine.api-target is set.
650	// For a GHES instance with api-target "api.acme.ghe.com", this returns both the API domain
651	// ("api.acme.ghe.com") and the base hostname ("acme.ghe.com") so that both the GitHub web UI
652	// and API requests pass through the firewall without manual lock file edits.
653	// Returns nil for empty apiTarget.
654	func GetAPITargetDomains(apiTarget string) []string {
655		if apiTarget == "" {
656			return nil
657		}
658	
659		domains := []string{apiTarget}
660	
661		// Derive the base hostname by stripping the first subdomain label, but only for
662		// API-style hostnames that start with "api.".
663		// e.g., "api.acme.ghe.com" → "acme.ghe.com"
664		// Only add the base hostname if it still looks like a multi-label hostname (contains a dot).
665		if strings.HasPrefix(apiTarget, "api.") {
666			if idx := strings.Index(apiTarget, "."); idx > 0 {
667				baseHost := apiTarget[idx+1:]
668				if strings.Contains(baseHost, ".") && baseHost != apiTarget {
669					domains = append(domains, baseHost)
670				}
671			}
672		}
673	
674		return domains
675	}

677	// mergeAPITargetDomains merges the api-target domains into an existing comma-separated domain string.
678	// When engine.api-target is set, both the API hostname and its base hostname are added to the allow-list.
679	// Returns the original string unchanged when apiTarget is empty.
680	func mergeAPITargetDomains(domainsStr string, apiTarget string) string {
681		extraDomains := GetAPITargetDomains(apiTarget)
682		if len(extraDomains) == 0 {
683			return domainsStr
684		}
685	
686		domainMap := make(map[string]bool)
687		for d := range strings.SplitSeq(domainsStr, ",") {
688			d = strings.TrimSpace(d)
689			if d != "" {
690				domainMap[d] = true
691			}
692		}
693		for _, d := range extraDomains {
694			domainMap[d] = true
695		}
696	
697		result := make([]string, 0, len(domainMap))
698		for d := range domainMap {
699			result = append(result, d)
700		}
701		sort.Strings(result)
702		return strings.Join(result, ",")
703	}

705	// computeAllowedDomainsForSanitization computes the allowed domains for sanitization
706	// based on the engine and network configuration, matching what's provided to the firewall
707	func (c *Compiler) computeAllowedDomainsForSanitization(data *WorkflowData) string {
708		// Determine which engine is being used
709		var engineID string
710		if data.EngineConfig != nil {
711			engineID = data.EngineConfig.ID
712		} else if data.AI != "" {
713			engineID = data.AI
714		}
715	
716		// Compute domains based on engine type, including tools and runtimes to match
717		// what's provided to the actual firewall at runtime
718		var base string
719		switch engineID {
720		case "copilot":
721			base = GetCopilotAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(data.NetworkPermissions, data.Tools, data.Runtimes)
722		case "codex":
723			base = GetCodexAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(data.NetworkPermissions, data.Tools, data.Runtimes)
724		case "claude":
725			base = GetClaudeAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(data.NetworkPermissions, data.Tools, data.Runtimes)
726		case "gemini":
727			base = GetGeminiAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(data.NetworkPermissions, data.Tools, data.Runtimes)
728		default:
729			// For other engines, use network permissions only
730			domains := GetAllowedDomains(data.NetworkPermissions)
731			base = strings.Join(domains, ",")
732		}
733	
734		// Add Copilot API target domains so GH_AW_ALLOWED_DOMAINS stays in sync with --allow-domains.
735		// Resolved from engine.api-target or GITHUB_COPILOT_BASE_URL in engine.env.
736		if copilotAPITarget := GetCopilotAPITarget(data); copilotAPITarget != "" {
737			base = mergeAPITargetDomains(base, copilotAPITarget)
738		}
739	
740		return base
741	}
742	
743	// expandAllowedDomains expands a list of domain entries (which may include ecosystem
744	// identifiers like "python", "node", "dev-tools") into a deduplicated, sorted list of
745	// concrete domain strings. This uses the same expansion logic as network.allowed.
746	func expandAllowedDomains(entries []string) []string {
747		domainMap := make(map[string]bool)
748		for _, entry := range entries {
749			ecosystemDomains := getEcosystemDomains(entry)
750			if len(ecosystemDomains) > 0 {
751				for _, d := range ecosystemDomains {
752					domainMap[d] = true
753				}
754			} else {
755				domainMap[entry] = true
756			}
757		}
758		result := make([]string, 0, len(domainMap))
759		for d := range domainMap {
760			result = append(result, d)
761		}
762		sort.Strings(result)
763		return result
764	}
765	
766	// computeExpandedAllowedDomainsForSanitization computes the allowed domains for URL sanitization,
767	// unioning the engine/network base set with the safe-outputs.allowed-domains entries.
768	// It always includes "localhost" and "github.com" in the result.
769	// The allowed-domains entries support ecosystem identifiers (same syntax as network.allowed).
770	func (c *Compiler) computeExpandedAllowedDomainsForSanitization(data *WorkflowData) string {
771		// Start from the base set (engine defaults + network.allowed + tools + runtimes)
772		base := c.computeAllowedDomainsForSanitization(data)
773	
774		domainMap := make(map[string]bool)
775	
776		// Seed from the base computation
777		if base != "" {
778			for d := range strings.SplitSeq(base, ",") {
779				d = strings.TrimSpace(d)
780				if d != "" {
781					domainMap[d] = true
782				}
783			}
784		}
785	
786		// Union with allowed-domains (expanded)
787		if data.SafeOutputs != nil && len(data.SafeOutputs.AllowedDomains) > 0 {
788			for _, d := range expandAllowedDomains(data.SafeOutputs.AllowedDomains) {
